gates.title: Production GitHub App named 6529bot has been created and configured from the reviewed GitHub App Registration Packet or equivalent manual settings.
Central App server and worker path are deployed in controlled 6529 infrastructure from a reviewed image or equivalent operator-reviewed runtime.
Provider keys, GitHub App secrets, AWS Data API access, and alerting secrets are configured only in the bot environment.
AWS IAM/OIDC trust and identity policies have been reviewed for the central bot runtime.
Reviewed central budget policies have been applied or budget control is explicitly limited to environment and repository caps for the release.
GitHub private vulnerability reporting is enabled for this repository or an equivalent private security intake channel is recorded in operator evidence.
The dashboard deployment plan has passed with explicit 6529.io origin, production bot origin, private operator workspace, auth-check URL, and require-ready evidence.
The merged 6529.io public transparency dashboard is deployed and wired to reviewed public repo/org disclosure allowlists.
The merged 6529.io private admin surface is deployed behind 6529 auth and wired to the HMAC admin auth bridge.
Scheduled operator alerts are enabled through reviewed private operator channels.
Trusted 6529 repository dogfood has run with conservative budgets, starting in command-only mode and advancing only after readiness, promotion, and go-live packets agree.
CI, Dependency Review, OpenSSF Scorecard, and manual security review have been completed for the release candidate.
Initial v0 tag plan has passed with explicit pre-v1 compatibility warnings and clean release notes.
README, changelog, release notes, install docs, and example configs match the tagged behavior.
